name: Waldren "Frog" Joseph
abstract: Waldren "Frog" Joseph (September 12, 1918 – September 19, 2004) was an American jazz trombone player from New Orleans, Louisiana.

about_quotes.speaker: Ellis Marsalis
about_quotes.evidence: Waldren Joseph was playing trombone. And we were doing like “Sensation Rag” — the traditional music.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ellis Marsalis interviewed by Monk Rowe, New Orleans, Louisiana, January 7, 2020

about_quotes.speaker: Geoff Bull
about_quotes.evidence: I played one set with him [Albert Burbank] around the Dixieland Hall... What band was he playing with at Dixieland Hall? Frog Joseph... It was Albert Burbank, Jack Willis, Frog Joseph.

about_quotes.speaker: Bernard Steinau
about_quotes.evidence: INTERVIEWER mentions Waldron Joseph came in as a substitute; INTERVIEWEE does not respond to this specific mention but moves on to discuss Myra Menville.

about_quotes.speaker: Sylvester Handy
about_quotes.evidence: We had another one... This same fella, Frog, we called him Frog... On trombone... He's still a sweet guy. Yeah, he's still playing nice.

about_quotes.speaker: William Russell
about_quotes.evidence: Frog Joseph was there I think maybe he knew the melody maybe he played the chorus the first

about_quotes.speaker: Geoff Bull
about_quotes.evidence: Well, I was surprisingly unimpressed with Frog Joseph for one.
